comparisons-and-truth-values.label: Comparisons and Truth Values
comparisons-and-truth-values.summary: Use comparison operators and understand True and False results.

help.concept: The `==` operator compares two values to see if they are equal.
help.hint_1: Think about how you would check if two numbers are the same.
help.hint_2: This operator returns `True` if both values are equal, otherwise it returns `False`.

options.a: Checks if two values are equal
options.b: Checks if two values are not equal
options.c: Compares the size of two values
options.d: Checks if one value is greater than the other

truthiness-in-python.title: Which of the following values are considered `False` in Python?
truthiness-in-python.hint: Focus on the main concept being tested and eliminate choices that describe something different.

help.concept: Choose the option that matches the core idea described in the question without relying on repeated wording from the answer choices.
help.hint_1: Rule out options that describe a different role, behavior, or concept than the question is testing.
help.hint_2: Pick the option that best matches the concept, not the one that simply repeats familiar wording.

fill-in-the-blank-true-false.title: Fill in the blank: The expression `5 > 3` evaluates to ___
fill-in-the-blank-true-false.prompt: What is the result of the expression?
fill-in-the-blank-true-false.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind fill in the blank: the expression `5 > 3` evaluates to ___.
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

compare-numbers.title: Compare Two Numbers
compare-numbers.prompt: Write a program that compares two numbers and prints whether the first is greater than, less than, or equal to the second.
compare-numbers.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

truthiness-check.title: Check Truthiness of a Value
truthiness-check.prompt: Write a program that checks if a value is truthy or falsy and prints the result.
truthiness-check.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.

help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

intro-to-comparisons.title: Introduction to Comparisons
intro-to-comparisons.bodyMarkdown: In Python, comparisons are made using operators like `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, and `>=`. These operators help us determine the relationship between values.

truth-values.title: Understanding Truth Values
truth-values.bodyMarkdown: In Python, every value has a truth value. For example, non-zero numbers and non-empty strings are considered `True`, while zero and empty strings are `False`.
